your_book:
Monday was a grind for Jaleel's book, which shed roughly 1.3% in unrealized terms on a $21,472 account — a modest but real drag with essentially no cash buffer (just $0.38). The session's standout mover to the downside was Rocket Lab (RKLB), off another 2.78% today and now sitting 28.1% in the hole — the deepest loss in the portfolio and rated AVOID with a QARP of just 45.5. Novartis (NVS) also took a notable hit, dropping 2.73% on the day; it carries an elevated drug-pricing policy overhang alongside Gilead. On the bright side, ServiceNow (NOW) gained 1.41% and Abbott (ABT) ticked up 0.97%, both delivering real contribution — though NOW has an earnings print due in just two days (July 22) that could swing things materially given its 11.2% weight and high multiple. Broadcom (AVGO) popped 1.98%, a welcome move for a small position, likely riding the broad semiconductor inflow narrative.

The drift that deserves honest scrutiny: ADBE ($3,678, 17.1% weight) is the book's largest holding, down 4.3% since entry and off 1.06% today, yet rated BUY — the thesis hasn't cracked but price and momentum are quietly separating. BSX is a similar story: rated STRONG BUY with a solid QARP of 82.9 but down 8.6% since entry, and guidance was slashed hard (FY26 growth cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5%). Both are cases where conviction is running ahead of the tape. LULU is also drifting — down 8.8% on the position, facing a guidance cut, Americas comps down five quarters running, and tariff pressure on margins.

Key things to watch into the week: NOW reports Tuesday (July 22) — a miss or cautious tone on AI deal pacing could hurt the book's second-largest weight materially. BSX reports July 29 with credibility already dented. And RKLB's AVOID rating combined with a 28.1% loss and 8.6% weight is a structural tension that doesn't resolve itself passively.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated position down 28.1% with no recovery thesis visible
attention.note: At 8.6% of the book, RKLB is the largest loser by far, rated AVOID with the portfolio's lowest QARP (45.5), and fell another 2.78% today — the system is flagging exit, and the numbers support that flag.

attention.headline: Earnings in 2 days on the book's #2 weight — no hedge, no cash
attention.note: ServiceNow reports July 22 and carries 11.2% of a fully-invested portfolio; a high multiple means any guidance miss or macro-sensitive tone could lead the drawdown with nothing to cushion it.

attention.headline: STRONG BUY thesis vs. slashed guidance and an 8.6% position loss
attention.note: Management guided flat through Q3 after cutting FY26 growth to 7–8.5%, yet the verdict remains STRONG BUY — with earnings July 29 approaching, the gap between rating and reality needs a hard look before the print.

attention.headline: Largest weight down 4.3% with narrative — not fundamentals — driving the slide
attention.note: At 17.1% of the book, ADBE's AI-disruption fear, CFO departure, and ARR de-emphasis are compressing the stock despite a beat-and-raise; price and thesis are drifting apart and the next catalyst isn't until September 10.
